states.name: Intake
states.context: Capture the customer's issue in plain language. Identify the product area, urgency, affected account, and the outcome the customer wants. Do not diagnose yet.

states.name: Clarify
states.context: Ask for the smallest missing detail needed to continue. Prefer one precise question over a long checklist.

states.name: Diagnose
states.context: Reason about likely causes using the available details. Produce one most likely cause and one alternative cause.

states.name: Resolve
states.context: Give the customer a clear next action or fix. Keep it actionable and avoid internal jargon.

states.name: Escalate
states.context: Prepare an escalation summary for a human specialist. Include customer impact, evidence gathered, attempted resolution, and why escalation is needed.

states.name: Close
states.context: Summarize the outcome, confirm the customer has a path forward, and state what was learned for future support.

associations.reason: the issue lacks required details such as product area, error message, account, or urgency
the issue has enough detail to infer likely causes
the missing detail has been supplied and the issue can now be reasoned about
the likely cause has a known customer-facing fix or workaround
the cause is unclear, risky, account-specific, or requires privileged access
the customer has a concrete answer or next step
the proposed fix fails or reveals a deeper system/account problem
the escalation package is complete and ready for handoff

testSessions.title: Quick transcript persistence test. Reply briefly.

messages.content: Quick transcript persistence test. Reply briefly.
Received — transcript persistence test acknowledged.
messages.stateReason: User asked for a brief reply; providing a short confirmation gives a concrete answer and lets us close the interaction.

testSessions.title: I cannot log in. It just keeps failing and I need this working s

messages.content: I cannot log in. It just keeps failing and I need this working soon.

I understand you're unable to log in and need this working soon. To help quickly, please provide these details:

- Which product/service/app/site are you trying to access?
- Which account (email or username) is affected?
- Exactly what happens when you try to log in? (Paste the exact error message or describe the behavior — e.g., “page reloads”, “invalid password”, “stuck on loading”, etc.)
- When did the problem start?
- What device, OS, and browser or app version are you using?
- Are you using a corporate SSO or single sign-on (Google/Microsoft/Okta)?
- Do you have MFA/2FA enabled? If so, what step fails?
- What troubleshooting have you already tried (password reset, cleared cache, different browser/device, etc.)?
- Can you share a screenshot or the exact error text if possible?
- How soon do you need this resolved (hours, by end of day, tomorrow)? Any deadlines or impacts we should know about?

Once you provide these, I can capture the issue and either escalate or guide next steps.
messages.stateReason: the issue lacks required details such as product area, error message, account, or urgency

messages.content: This is for Acme Ops, account ACME-1042. The app says invalid session after SSO, Chrome on Windows, started this morning, affects three users.

Thanks — that’s helpful. Quick checklist of immediate checks I can help with, plus one small question so I can prioritize diagnostics:

Immediate steps you can try now (fast):
- Have an affected user try Chrome incognito (or another browser) to see if the problem persists.
- Clear cookies for your Acme Ops domain and retry SSO.
- Check the browser DevTools → Network for the SSO callback response and whether the app’s session cookie is being Set (and whether it’s blocked due to SameSite or Secure rules).

If those don’t help, I’ll walk through server/IdP checks: verify the IdP’s assertion success logs, confirm the app is creating a session on the SSO callback, check for recent deploys or load-balancer/sticky-session changes, and confirm server and client clocks are in sync.

One quick question to narrow the root cause: which SSO/IdP are you using (Okta, Azure AD/Microsoft, Google, ADFS, or other)?
messages.stateReason: User supplied product, account, error text, browser/OS, start time, and scope (three users), so we have enough to begin diagnostic steps.

  "assistantMessage": "Thanks — that’s helpful. Quick checklist of immediate checks I can help with, plus one small question so I can prioritize diagnostics:

Immediate steps you can try now (fast):
- Have an affected user try Chrome incognito (or another browser) to see if the problem persists.
- Clear cookies for your Acme Ops domain and retry SSO.
- Check the browser DevTools → Network for the SSO callback response and whether the app’s session cookie is being Set (and whether it’s blocked due to SameSite or Secure rules).

If those don’t help, I’ll walk through server/IdP checks: verify the IdP’s assertion success logs, confirm the app is creating a session on the SSO callback, check for recent deploys or load-balancer/sticky-session changes, and confirm server and client clocks are in sync.

One quick question to narrow the root cause: which SSO/IdP are you using (Okta, Azure AD/Microsoft, Google, ADFS, or other)?",
  "nextStateId": 3,
  "nextStateName": "Diagnose",
  "stateReason": "User supplied product, account, error text, browser/OS, start time, and scope (three users), so we have enough to begin diagnostic steps."
}

messages.content: We cleared cache and tried a private window. Same error. Password reset did not help either.

Most likely cause: The app’s session cookie is being blocked or not persisted after the cross-site SSO callback (e.g., SameSite/Secure cookie attributes missing or rejected by Chrome), so the app never receives a usable session and reports “invalid session after SSO.” Rationale: SSO uses a cross-site redirect where browsers will block cookies that lack SameSite=None; Secure or have incompatible attributes — this fits an abrupt, multi-user failure that client-side cache clears/private windows don’t fix.

Alternative cause: Server-side session persistence or verification failure (session store like Redis/memcached down, DB/redis errors, or a session-signing/key rotation or recent deploy that broke session creation), so the app receives the SSO assertion but fails to create/validate a session.

Immediate checks you can run now (fast):
- In Chrome DevTools during the SSO flow, inspect the SSO callback response and check the Set-Cookie header for the session cookie: confirm it is present and has SameSite=None and Secure (and correct Domain/Path). Also confirm the cookie is sent on the subsequent request back to the app.
- Look for cookie warnings in the Console (Chrome will warn if a cookie is blocked due to SameSite).
- Try a different browser (Firefox) or device to see if behavior differs (helps distinguish browser-block vs server-side). You already tried private window which is helpful.
- Check the app logs for the SSO callback handling for those users (look for errors creating sessions, exceptions, or 5xx responses) and check your session store health (Redis/memcached) and application errors around the time the users attempted login.
- Confirm any recent deploys, secret/key rotations, or config changes (load-balancer sticky session changes) since this morning.

What I need from you to proceed or to escalate: which IdP are you using (Okta / Azure AD / Google / ADFS / other)? Please capture and share the SSO callback request/response (network/Set-Cookie headers) or a screenshot of the cookie warning and any relevant app log lines (time, user id) showing the failed session creation.

Next step: This looks like it requires access to server logs, session-store health, and possibly config or deploy history to confirm and remediate. I recommend escalating to your SRE/ops team (or I can escalate for you) so they can inspect server logs, session-store, and cookie configuration and apply the fix (e.g., set SameSite=None; Secure or restore session-store).
messages.stateReason: The likely causes require privileged access to server logs, session-store health, or configuration changes (cookie attributes or deploy/secret rollbacks), so this should be escalated to SRE/ops for investigation and remediation.

messages.content: Yes, please escalate. I can share user IDs, timestamp, browser version, and screenshots for the support handoff.

Escalation summary for human specialist — please triage and remediate

Affected customer
- Customer: Acme Ops
- Account: ACME-1042
- Scope: 3 users currently unable to log in via SSO
- Start time: this morning (user will provide exact timestamps)
- Environment: Chrome on Windows (user will provide exact Chrome version); problem reproduced in private/incognito and after cache/cookie clears
- Urgency: High — users cannot access the app; customer requests resolution “soon” (please treat as high-priority SLA)

Observed behavior & evidence gathered
- Symptom: After completing SSO, the app shows “invalid session after SSO” (session is not persisted after SSO callback)
- Client-side troubleshooting performed by customer: cleared cache, used private/incognito window, password reset — none resolved the issue
- Likely causes identified by initial investigation:
  - Browser blocking/persistence of session cookie after cross-site SSO callback (SameSite/Secure attributes missing or rejected by recent Chrome behavior)
  - Server-side session creation/persistence failure (e.g., session store like Redis down, session signing/key rotation, app exception during SSO callback)
- Diagnostic suggestions already provided to the customer (they can capture these for handoff):
  - Capture DevTools Network trace for SSO callback and confirm Set-Cookie headers and whether cookie is actually sent on subsequent requests
  - Capture any Console warnings about blocked cookies (SameSite warnings)
  - Provide app server logs for the SSO callback and session creation for the affected users (timestamps and user IDs)
  - Check session-store health/metrics (Redis/memcached errors, latency, connection errors)
  - Confirm recent deploys, config changes (cookie config, load-balancer sticky sessions), or key/secret rotations since this morning

Attempted/resolved steps by support agent and customer
- Customer tried: cache/cookie clear, private/incognito window, password reset
- Support agent recommended: DevTools inspection for Set-Cookie and cookie warnings, check server SSO handling logs, check session store and deploys. Customer has agreed to escalate and can provide additional artifacts.

Why escalation is needed
- Requires access beyond end-user troubleshooting: application logs at SSO callback, session-store diagnostics, load-balancer/sticky-session configs, and IdP assertion logs. Potential fixes may include config change (cookie attributes), session-store remediation, or rollback of a recent deploy. These actions require SRE/app-ownership privileges.

Immediate next actions for on-call specialist (checklist)
1. Collect artifacts from customer (requested below). Attach to ticket.
2. Examine IdP/IdP-proxy logs for SAML/OIDC assertions for the provided user IDs and timestamps; confirm assertion success and timestamps.
3. Inspect application logs at the exact SSO callback times for exceptions, 5xxs, or errors when creating sessions. Note any stack traces or error messages.
4. Inspect HTTP responses for Set-Cookie on the SSO callback response:
   - Confirm session cookie is Set
   - Confirm attributes: SameSite=None; Secure; Domain and Path correct; HttpOnly as expected
   - Confirm cookie is present on subsequent request(s) from browser
5. Review session-store (Redis/memcached) status and recent error logs around the timestamps; check for OOMs, connection errors, or ACL/credential changes
6. Review recent deploy/change logs (last 24 hours) for changes to session handling, cookie configuration, load balancer, or key rotations
7. If cookie attributes are incorrect, push config fix (SameSite=None; Secure) and coordinate a rollback or config change as appropriate
8. If session-store is unhealthy, remediate/restore and consider temporarily enabling alternate session persistence or failover
9. If IdP assertion issues are found, coordinate with IdP team (Okta/Azure/Google) — confirm which IdP in ticket
10. Reproduce with a test account and capture DevTools network trace to confirm fix

What we need from the customer to complete the handoff (please attach to the ticket)
- Exact user IDs (all 3 affected users)
- Exact timestamps of failed login attempts (with timezone) for each user
- Exact Chrome version string (from chrome://version) and Windows build if possible
- Screenshots or exported HAR of the SSO flow showing the SSO callback response and Set-Cookie headers (Network tab) and any Console SameSite cookie warnings
- Any relevant app log snippets (time, request id if present, stack traces) for the SSO callback and attempted session creation
- Confirmation of IdP in use (Okta / Azure AD / Google / ADFS / other) and whether any recent IdP changes occurred
- Confirmation of any recent deploys, config changes, or session-key/secret rotations this morning

Recommended ticket metadata
- Priority: P1 / High
- Suggested assignees: SRE on-call + Application owner + IdP integration owner
- Attach: HAR/screenshot, user IDs+timestamps, app logs, session-store metrics

Customer communication suggested wording (to send after triage)
- "We’re escalating this to SRE and the app team to investigate session persistence and the SSO callback. Please provide us the requested HAR/screenshots and timestamps so we can correlate with server logs. We’ll update you as soon as we have a root cause or mitigation."

Contact / next steps with this assistant
- Customer is ready to attach user IDs, timestamps, browser version, and screenshots. Please confirm preferred secure upload method (support ticket upload, SFTP, or email to ops) and we will attach to this escalation.

Prepared by: Support agent (initial triage) — please pick up from here and perform server-side investigation and remediation.
messages.stateReason: Customer confirmed escalation and can provide additional artifacts; the escalation package is prepared but awaiting the customer's user IDs, timestamps, HAR/screenshots, and any app logs before the handoff can be closed.

states.name: Receive
states.context: Capture the customer's return request. Identify the order, product, reason, timing, condition, and requested outcome. Do not decide eligibility yet.

states.name: Validate
states.context: Check whether the request has enough information to evaluate policy eligibility. Ask only for missing facts needed to continue.

states.name: Assess
states.context: Evaluate the request against return policy signals: purchase date, item category, condition, defect claims, and proof of purchase.

states.name: Approve
states.context: Approve the return and provide clear next steps for shipping, refund timing, replacement, or store credit.

states.name: Deny
states.context: Decline the return respectfully. Explain the policy reason and offer alternatives when appropriate.

states.name: Escalate
states.context: Prepare a human review package for edge cases, high-value orders, fraud concerns, or policy exceptions.

states.name: Close
states.context: Summarize the decision, next step, and any follow-up the customer should expect.

associations.reason: the request lacks order, product, timing, condition, or requested outcome details
the request includes enough order and product details to evaluate eligibility
missing facts have been supplied and policy eligibility can now be evaluated
the request appears eligible under the return policy
the request clearly violates return policy and no exception is indicated
the request involves an exception, high-value item, fraud signal, or unclear policy edge
the customer has return authorization and concrete next steps
the customer has a clear policy explanation and alternatives
the human review package is complete and ready for handoff

testSessions.title: I want to return headphones I bought recently. The right side st

messages.content: I want to return headphones I bought recently. The right side stopped working and I need a refund.

Thanks — I can help with that. To get started I need a few details so I can verify the order and check return eligibility: 1) Order number or the email/phone used to place the order; 2) Exact headphone model/name (or a photo of the product/label); 3) Purchase date (or how many days/weeks ago you bought them) and where you bought them (our site, a marketplace, or a physical store); 4) A photo or short video showing the right side not working (audio or physical damage); 5) Whether you’ve tried basic troubleshooting (reset, charge, firmware update) and the results; 6) Whether you still have the original packaging and accessories; 7) Confirmation you want a refund (you mentioned refund) or if you’d accept a replacement. Provide whichever of the above you have and I’ll check eligibility and tell you the next steps.
messages.stateReason: the request lacks order, product, timing, condition, or requested outcome details

messages.content: Order RMA-77821, purchased 12 days ago. They are wireless headphones, no visible damage, and I still have the box and receipt.

Thanks — I have Order RMA-77821 and see it was purchased 12 days ago. With the box and receipt and no visible damage, this looks like it would fall within our return window and is likely eligible for a refund for a defective unit. Before I finalize the return authorization I just need two quick things: 1) A short video (or audio clip) showing the right side not working (power/audio issue) so we can confirm the defect; 2) Whether you’ve tried basic troubleshooting (reset/re-pair, fully charged battery, firmware update) and the results. If you’ve already tried those steps, tell me what happened. Once you send the video and troubleshooting results I’ll approve the return and email a prepaid return label and instructions. After we receive and inspect the headphones we’ll issue the refund to the original payment method (typically processed within 5–10 business days). Shall I proceed?
messages.stateReason: The user provided the order number, purchase timing, condition, and receipt—enough information to begin eligibility evaluation—so move from Validate to Assess to determine and complete the return authorization steps.

messages.content: A replacement is fine if it ships quickly. I can print a label today.

Thanks — replacement is fine. I can prioritize a replacement once we confirm the defect. To proceed please: 1) Send a short video (or audio clip) that demonstrates the right side not working (play audio with both sides, show no output on the right, or show any power/connection behavior). 2) Tell me which troubleshooting steps you’ve already tried (reset/re-pair, fully charged battery, firmware update) and the results. 3) Confirm whether you want expedited shipping for the replacement. You said you can print a label — once I review the video and troubleshooting results I’ll issue the RMA and email a prepaid return label and instructions. After we receive and inspect the headphones we’ll ship the replacement (typically ships within 1–3 business days after we receive and inspect the return; expedited requests will be prioritized if available). Send the video and troubleshooting details and I’ll move this forward.
messages.stateReason: Still need defect evidence (video/audio) and troubleshooting results to confirm eligibility and finalize the replacement authorization before approving the return.

states.name: Report
states.context: Capture the incident report in plain language. Identify what happened, where, who is affected, urgency, and immediate safety concerns.

states.name: Clarify
states.context: Ask for the smallest missing detail needed to classify severity and choose a response path.

states.name: Classify
states.context: Classify severity and incident type using available details. Distinguish safety, facilities, attendee conduct, technical, and logistics issues.

states.name: Respond
states.context: Provide immediate response actions appropriate for the incident type. Keep actions concrete and time-sensitive.

states.name: Escalate
states.context: Prepare an escalation summary for security, medical, venue operations, or event leadership.

states.name: Document
states.context: Record the incident facts, response taken, unresolved risks, and required follow-up.

states.name: Close
states.context: Confirm the incident has a resolution path and summarize what should be monitored next.

associations.reason: the incident lacks location, affected people, severity, or immediate risk details
the incident report includes enough facts to classify severity and type
missing incident facts have been supplied
the incident can be handled with standard immediate response actions
the incident involves safety risk, medical need, security concern, or leadership decision
initial response actions have been provided and the event record should be updated
the response reveals continuing risk or requires authority beyond the operator
the escalation package has enough detail for handoff and recordkeeping
the incident facts and follow-up have been captured

testSessions.title: There is a problem near the east entrance. People are backing up

messages.content: There is a problem near the east entrance. People are backing up and someone said a guest fell.

Got it — problem near the east entrance, people are backing up, and someone reportedly fell. I need a few more details to help and to know if immediate emergency action is required.

Immediate safety (if you are there):
- Make sure the area is safe for you to approach (no hazards like traffic, spilled liquids, or falling objects).
- If the fallen person is unresponsive or not breathing, call emergency services now and begin CPR if trained.
- If they are bleeding heavily, apply direct pressure and call for medical help.
- Do not move the person unless they are in immediate danger.

Questions: 
1) Is the fallen guest conscious and breathing? 
2) Are there signs of major bleeding, head injury, or severe pain? 
3) How many people are affected or involved? 
4) Is the east entrance inside or outside which building/area (exact location)? 
5) Are you on scene and able to assist or call security/EMS? 

Please answer these so I can classify the incident and advise next steps.
messages.stateReason: Report lacks key details (victim condition, severity, exact location, number affected, and whether immediate medical or security help is needed); clarifying questions and safety guidance are required.

messages.content: East entrance gate 3. One attendee tripped on a loose floor mat, is sitting up, and says their ankle hurts. The line is blocking the doorway.

Thanks — that’s enough to classify. Preliminary classification: likely a low-to-moderate injury (probable ankle sprain) and not immediately life-threatening based on: conscious, sitting up, localized ankle pain, no major bleeding reported.

Immediate actions to take now:
- Keep the attendee seated and still. Do not let them put weight on the ankle.
- Check distal circulation and sensation: ask them to wiggle toes and say if they feel normal; check for pale/cold foot or weak/absent pulse. If numbness, loss of pulse, severe deformity, or worsening severe pain, call emergency services immediately. 
- Apply first aid: ice wrapped in cloth to the ankle (15–20 minutes), elevate the leg if possible, and consider a light compression bandage only if no obvious fracture and you are trained to do so. Stop and request EMS if a fracture is suspected.
- Clear the doorway: ask staff/security to have people step back or reroute the line to keep the entrance clear for access and to prevent further trips. Move the fallen person only if they are in immediate danger or you are trained and have appropriate support.
- Call on-site medical/first aid or security to assess and assist with moving the attendee to a safe area for further assessment/transport. If the attendee cannot bear weight or pain/swelling is severe, arrange transport to urgent care/ER.
- Secure the hazard: remove or tape down the loose floor mat or close the entrance until the mat is fixed, and log witness names for the incident record.

If you want, tell me whether the attendee can wiggle toes and whether the foot looks deformed or pale/cold — I can then recommend whether EMS is needed or help you draft an on-site response to send to security/medical.
messages.stateReason: Missing incident facts have been supplied (location, mechanism, condition of the person), so we can classify severity and choose a response path.

messages.content: Medical staff are on the way and security is moving the line. Venue ops needs the loose mat location and incident notes.

Loose mat location: East Entrance — Gate 3. Mat located at the doorway threshold on the interior side of the entrance (remove/tape down or close the gate until fixed).

Incident notes (for venue ops):
- Incident type: Trip/fall due to loose floor mat (facility hazard).
- Location: East Entrance, Gate 3 (at the interior threshold of the doorway).
- Brief description: One attendee tripped on a loose floor mat, is sitting up and reports ankle pain. No major bleeding or loss of consciousness reported. Medical staff en route; security is moving the line to clear the doorway.
- Current condition: conscious, seated, localized ankle pain (possible sprain). Confirm weight-bearing ability and distal circulation/sensation when medical staff assess.
- On-scene actions taken: security rerouting the line; medical responding; area being cleared for access. (Confirm whether attendee has been kept off the injured leg, provided ice/elevation, or received any first aid.)

Immediate venue ops actions recommended:
- Dispatch maintenance immediately to remove or secure the loose mat (tape down or replace) and inspect the threshold for other hazards.
- Temporarily close or redirect the entrance until the mat is secured.
- Place warning/temporary barrier to prevent further trips while repairing.

Evidence and documentation to collect:
- Photos of mat and incident area (wide and close-up), and of any footwear/foot position if appropriate and with consent.
- CCTV clip covering the entrance timestamped to the incident.
- Names and contact details of the injured attendee, security and medical responders, and witnesses.
- Time of incident and time medical/security were notified, and any first aid provided.

Follow-up actions:
- Log a maintenance corrective action and schedule a preventive inspection of other entrance mats/thresholds.
- Follow up with the injured attendee about outcome (transport to urgent care/ER if needed) and retain incident report in records.

Do you want me to format this into a formal incident report template you can send to venue ops (including placeholders for time, attendee name, responder names, and photos)?
messages.stateReason: Medical staff are en route and security is addressing crowding; the issue is a facility hazard that can be handled with immediate response actions (securing the mat, documenting the incident, and coordinating maintenance and follow-up).

messages.content: Medical took over, security cleared the doorway, and venue ops removed the mat. Please document it for follow-up.

Incident follow-up record (for venue ops & incident log)

Incident ID: [assign ID]
Reported by: [your name/contact]
Date: [YYYY-MM-DD]
Time of incident: [HH:MM]
Location: East Entrance — Gate 3 (interior threshold)

Incident summary:
- Type: Trip/fall due to loose floor mat (facility hazard)
- Description: One attendee tripped on a loose floor mat at the interior threshold of Gate 3. Attendee was sitting up and reported ankle pain when first assessed. No loss of consciousness or major bleeding reported.

Immediate actions taken (on-scene):
- Security: cleared the doorway/line to allow access and prevent further trips.
- Medical: on-site medical staff responded and assumed care of the attendee (transfer of care noted).
- Venue Operations/Maintenance: removed/secured the loose mat at Gate 3 immediately.

Status at handoff:
- Attendee: under care of medical staff (medical to record outcome/transport details).
- Hazard: mat removed and entrance cleared; temporary measures implemented.

Evidence to collect and attach:
- Photos of the incident area (wide and close-up of the threshold where mat was located) — collected: [yes/no]
- CCTV clip of the entrance covering the incident timestamp — collected: [yes/no]
- Names and contact info: injured attendee, security staff on scene, medical responders, maintenance staff, witnesses — list below

On-scene personnel (please fill):
- Injured attendee name/contact: [ ]
- Security responder(s): [ ]
- Medical responder(s): [ ]
- Maintenance staff who removed mat: [ ]
- Witness(es): [ ]

Times (please fill):
- Time reported to staff: [HH:MM]
- Time security arrived/cleared line: [HH:MM]
- Time medical arrived/took over: [HH:MM]
- Time maintenance removed mat: [HH:MM]

Recommended follow-up actions (owners and target dates):
1) Medical: document treatment provided, transport decision, and final condition; provide copy to incident log. (Owner: Medical) — Due: immediate
2) Venue Ops/Maintenance: verify permanent fix for threshold/mat (replace or secure with non-slip system), confirm no other mats/thresholds present similar hazard, and close maintenance ticket. (Owner: Venue Ops/Maintenance) — Due: within 24 hrs
3) Security: provide witness statements and confirm crowd control/reroute actions taken. (Owner: Security) — Due: same shift
4) Evidence collection: export CCTV and upload photos to incident record. (Owner: Ops/IC) — Due: within 24 hrs
5) Incident review: conduct root-cause review and schedule preventive inspection of all entrance mats/thresholds. (Owner: Ops Safety) — Due: 7 days
6) Follow-up with attendee: confirm medical outcome and any claims or further needs; file closure note. (Owner: Incident Reporter / Guest Relations) — Due: 48–72 hrs

Closure criteria:
- Medical outcome documented and attached
- CCTV/photos uploaded
- Maintenance ticket completed and permanent fix confirmed
- Witness statements captured
- Preventive action scheduled

Notes / Additional comments:
- If the attendee was transported to urgent care/ER, attach transport details and receiving facility name.
- If injury escalates or liability concern is raised, escalate to Legal/Risk per policy.

Please confirm the missing fields (names, times, evidence collected) and I will produce a finalized incident report to file.
messages.stateReason: initial response actions have been provided and the event record should be updated
